{"query":"Swedish Academy Han Kang 2024 Nobel Prize Literature official bio bibliography specific books cited","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1878,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/literature/2024/han/facts/","title":"Han Kang – Facts – 2024 - NobelPrize.org","content":"In her oeuvre, she confronts historical traumas and invisible sets of rules and, in each of her works, exposes the fragility of human life.
Among her works are The Vegetarian, Human Acts and We Do Not Part.
Here Steve Sem-Sandberg from the Swedish Academy gives his recommendations."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Nobel_Prize_in_Literature","title":"2024 Nobel Prize in Literature - Wikipedia","content":"It was announced by the Swedish Academy in Stockholm, Sweden, on 10 October 2024 and was awarded on 10 December 2024.
Han Kang grew up with a literary background in Seoul, her father, Han Seung-won, being a reputed novelist.
She stated that she was "honored" to receive the prize.
And Human Acts is connected directly with this book We Do Not Part.
Discussing the choice of Han Kang as the Nobel laureate on Sveriges Television minutes after the announcement, a panel of the Swedish literature critics Ingrid Elam, Jonas Thente and author Lyra Ekström Lindbäck disagreed on the Swedish Academy's choice.
The choice of Han Kang was well received by several German commentators, who found it a good choice and positive that the Swedish Academy after heavy focus on European literature in recent years had chosen a writer from another part of the world.
BTS member V shared in the news on Instagram, writing: "I read your book 'Human Act: A Novel' in the army.
A number of South Korean writers celebrated the news, among them Min Jin Lee, author of Pachinko, who commented: "Han Kang is a brilliant novelist who reflects our modern condition with courage, imagination, and intelligence.
Han Kang delivered her Nobel lecture on 7 December 2024 at the Swedish Academy in Stockholm.
"Han Kang of South Korea wins Nobel literature prize 2024".
"Ett ganska svagt val av Svenska Akademien" (in Swedish).
South Korean Nobel win sparks joy, hope at home"."},{"url":"https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/literature/2024/han/lecture/","title":"Han Kang – Nobel Prize lecture - NobelPrize.org","content":"This was in the spring of 2012, not long after Greek Lessons was published.
I was nine years old when my family left Gwangju in January 1980, roughly four months before the mass killings began.
I obtained a book containing more than 900 testimonials, and every day for nine hours over the course of a month, I read each account collected there.
Then I read up on not just Gwangju but other cases of state violence.
Just as in this novel, Human Acts, the child Dong-ho tugs at his mother’s hand to coax her towards the sun.
Finally, in December 2017, I rented a room on Jeju Island and spent the next two years or so dividing my time between Jeju and Seoul.
If the first part is a horizontal journey that follows the narrator, Kyungha, from Seoul to her friend Inseon’s home in the Jeju uplands through heavy snow towards the pet bird she has been tasked with saving, then the second part follows a vertical path that leads Kyungha and Inseon down to one of humanity’s darkest nights – to the winter of 1948 when civilians on Jeju were slaughtered – and into the ocean’s depths."},{"url":"https://www.publishersweekly.com/pw/by-topic/industry-news/awards-and-prizes/article/96167-han-kang-wins-2024-nobel-prize-in-literature.html","title":"Han Kang Wins 2024 Nobel Prize in Literature","content":"Oct 10, 2024 — The Vegetarian (2015), Human Acts (2016), The White Book (2019), Europa (2019), and Greek Lessons (2023)—and"}]}

In 2024, the Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded to the South Korean author Han Kang (born 1970) \"for her intense poetic prose that confronts historical traumas and exposes the fragility of human life\". It was announced by the Swedish Academy in Stockholm, Sweden, on 10 October 2024 and was awarded on 10 December 2024. She is the first South Korean and first Asian woman to win the Nobel Prize in Literature, making her the 18th woman to win the Nobel Prize in Literature and the tenth Asian laureate in that Nobel Prize category. She started her career in 1993 with the publication of her poems in the literary magazine *Literature and Society*, and had her debut prose publication in 1995 with the short story collection *Love of Yeosu* (여수의 사랑).","score":0.76173395,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://theconversation.com/han-kang-innovative-south-korean-author-wins-the-2024-nobel-prize-for-literature-241088","title":"Han Kang: innovative South Korean author wins the 2024 Nobel prize for literature","content":"# **Han Kang: innovative South Korean author wins the 2024 Nobel prize for literature**. Han Kang’s The Vegetarian (2007) is a case in point, and it is no doubt the work that was most influential in the Swedish Academy’s decision to award her the Nobel prize in literature 2024. The committee stated that Kang was awarded the prestigious prize because her “poetic and experimental style” has made her “an innovator in contemporary prose”. Han Kang is the first South Korean writer to be awarded the Nobel prize in literature, and in its history of 121 winners over 117 years, only the 18th woman to be awarded the prize. Published in 2007, and translated into English for publication in the UK in 2015 and the US in 2016, its title was apt, as it coincided with a sudden upsurge in people turning to vegetarianism and veganism, particularly in the UK.","score":0.7251239,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.writerswrite.com/han-kang-nobel-prize-101020241","title":"Han Kang Wins The Nobel Prize in Literature for 2024","content":"Kang is the first South Korean winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature.
